+ My favorite black-box optimizer - - If you have gradients, use them. In the many places where gradients don’t exist (aircraft - wings, bridges, neural architecture search), CMA-ES quietly shines. This is a straight - walkthrough of why it works, how it morphs a Gaussian into an inverse-Hessian stand‑in, - and how to run it yourself in the browser or in Python without touching a single - derivative. - - - Roots: CMA-ES grew out of evolution strategies (Rechenberg/Schwefel) and split from - classic genetic algorithms by optimizing the search distribution instead of - chromosomes. It shares DNA with kriging’s “neutral prior, bend with evidence” mindset and - the estimation-of-distribution / cross-entropy family. - +
+ + If you have gradients, use them. But when gradients don’t exist—like in aircraft wing design, bridge engineering, or neural architecture search—CMA-ES quietly shines. + + + This is a walkthrough of why it works, how it morphs a Gaussian into an inverse-Hessian stand‑in, and how to run it yourself without touching a single derivative. + +

+ Rust CMA-ES compiled to WebAssembly, wrapped in a clean JS/TS API and paired with a D3 + Tailwind playground. Drop a serious optimizer into any browser tool and watch the covariance ellipsoid adapt live.

-

- A hyper-optimized Rust core with a friendly Python surface. Feels like - scipy.optimize, runs like a modern ES: SIMD, Rayon, deterministic seeds, - restarts, constraints, vectorized objectives, and a Rich TUI. + +

+ A hyper-optimized Rust core with a friendly Python surface. Feels like scipy.optimize, runs like a modern ES: SIMD, Rayon, deterministic seeds, restarts, constraints, and a Rich TUI.

-
    -
  • PyPI: fast-cmaes (module fastcma); wheels for Linux/macOS/Windows.
    • -
  • APIs: fmin, fmin_vec, fmin_constrained, and a configurable CMAES class.
    • -
  • Full or diagonal covariance, restart helpers, seeded runs, optional LAPACK eigensolver and NumPy fast paths.
    • -
  • Rich-powered terminal UI to watch σ, best-f, and eval counts live.
    • -
-

Minimal Python usage:

- 
-{`python -m pip install fast-cmaes

-def sphere(x):

- Build/dev loop: maturin develop --release (optionally --features numpy_support,eigen_lapack), or run - ./scripts/setup_and_demo.sh to spin up a uv env and launch the Rich TUI. -

-

- Practical limit note: full CMA-ES is O(n²) inside; switch to the diagonal mode for cheap, - very high-dimensional problems, or keep full covariance when each evaluation is costly and - geometry learning matters most. -

-
- - Practical playbook: constraints, noise, budgets -
-
-
-
Constraints & bounds
-
    -
  • Work in an unconstrained space; logit/tanh to map back; or clip/reflect at bounds.
    • -
  • Categories: carve [0,1] into intervals, quantize late; keeps search smoother.
    • -
  • Hard constraints: add rank-based penalties; repair samples instead of rejecting.
    • -
+
+
+
+ + Practical Playbook: Constraints, Noise, Budgets
-
-
Noise & budgets
-
    -
  • For noisy f: enlarge λ, reevaluate elites, average top-k, or use active updates to shrink bad directions.
    • -
  • Budgeting: λ ≈ 4 + floor(3 log n); expect ~50–200 generations; restart if stalled.
    • -
  • Keep seeds and ask/tell logs so you can replay and debug; determinism saves days.
    • -
+ +
+
+
Constraints & Bounds
+
    +
  • Work in an unconstrained space; logit/tanh to map back; or clip/reflect at bounds.
    • +
  • Categories: carve [0,1] into intervals, quantize late; keeps search smoother.
    • +
  • Hard constraints: add rank-based penalties; repair samples instead of rejecting.
    • +
+
+ +
+
Noise & Budgets
+
    +
  • For noisy f: enlarge λ, reevaluate elites, average top-k, or use active updates.
    • +
  • Budgeting: λ ≈ 4 + floor(3 log n); expect ~50–200 generations; restart if stalled.
    • +
  • Keep seeds and ask/tell logs so you can replay and debug; determinism saves days.
    • +
+
+
+ +
+ + Rule of thumb: If each eval costs hours, invest in full covariance.

Practical tricks

-
    -
  • Reflect or squash out-of-bounds samples rather than rejecting them to keep data flowing.
    • -
  • Quantize categorical dimensions as late as possible so the search stays continuous.
    • -
  • Keep seeds and ask/tell logs so you can replay a run; determinism makes debugging sane.
    • -
